when_to_use: loop-based, dusty and warm, developed by subtraction/addition, 75-95 BPM, Rhodes+fat bass pocket

defaults.feel: swung-16th, deep pocket, dusty and relaxed

signature_moves: 2-4 chord loop with tasteful reharm on repeats (change the top voice, keep the bass)
borrowed color: Dm9 -> Bbmaj7 -> Ebmaj7#11 (bVI) -> A7alt
chromatic bass walkup under a held upper voicing
cadence_options: loop-point turnaround: Am7 -> D9 -> Gm11 (back to top)
deceptive: A7alt -> Bbmaj7 instead of Dm
avoid: chord density as a substitute for pocket - identity is groove+texture+space (see style-cheatsheets Lofi jazz)
the exact same voicing looped with zero reharm for the whole tune (see ../references/cliche-register.md)

hook_archetypes.name: short-motif-loop
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a 2-bar motif that lands on the '&' of 2, leaving space after
hook_archetypes.contour: a small falling gesture (3rd down) that answers itself an octave apart on the repeat

hook_archetypes.name: top-voice-hook
hook_archetypes.rhythm: sustained top note of the comp voicing, re-struck on the reharm
hook_archetypes.contour: the color tone (9th or #11) held so the reharm underneath changes its meaning
hook_archetypes.first_appears: the second loop, when the reharm hits

settle.note_lengths: sparse, mostly space; let the loop breathe
settle.contour: small gestures around the tonic and the color tone
settle.placement: deep in the pocket, behind the beat

develop.note_lengths: add one phrase per loop; build by addition
develop.contour: reach a little higher, introduce the reharm color tone

lift.note_lengths: the fullest the tune gets - still restrained
lift.contour: hold the highest color tone over the loop's reharm
lift.placement: in the pocket, a touch more forward

dissolve.note_lengths: strip back by subtraction to almost nothing
dissolve.contour: settle onto the tonic or the loop's most stable tone

drum_skeleton.low_energy: dusty kick on 1 and the 'a' of 2, laid-back snare on 2 and 4, sparse hat, lots of space
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: add swung 16th hat ghosts (vel < 45), a syncopated kick echoing the bass riff
drum_skeleton.high_energy: still restrained - add a light open-hat accent and a short fill at the loop point, never busy

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: develop the single loop by subtraction/addition, not by adding sections; strip to keys+bass for a breakdown; lead enters late and lays out often to keep the pocket the focus

anti_boredom_rules: each loop repeat must differ from the last by subtraction, addition, OR reharm - never an identical bar-for-bar copy
the reharm must land at least once so a held top voice changes meaning
no 2-bar loop repeats more than 4 times without a change
velocity is never flat across a voice within a bar (dusty ghosts low, hits higher)
keep space - do not fill every beat; the pocket is the identity
